name: Thai Satay
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Beef sirloin tip or top round, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub beef with garlic, pressing garlic into the meat.
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and sugar. Cut the beef into 1-inch pieces; thread onto skewers. Set aside.
3. Combine lemon juice and soy sauce; brush over the meat.
4. Grill or broil at a distance of 3 to 4 inches from the heat until browned on all sides.
Serving:
- Serve with Satay Sauce (makes 4 to 6 servings)
- Pork Satay: Use lean pork instead of beef; season with 1/2 teaspoon powdered ginger and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon in addition to pepper.
- Satay Sauce: Combine 2 cloves of garlic, crushed, 4 tablespoons peanut butter, 1 tablespoon vinegar or lemon juice, 3 tablespoons soy sauce, 1/4 teaspoon cayenne, and 4 tablespoons hot water (1/4 cup). Stir until well blended.
- Cucumber Condiment: Combine 1 firm, medium-sized cucumber, 2 tablespoons distilled vinegar, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon ground white pepper, and 1/2 small yellow onion, peeled, sliced lengthwise into paper thin slivers. Add 12 paper-thin, seeded hot red chile peppers, about 1/4 inch long.
- Garnish: 1/2 cup dry roasted unsalted peanuts, coarsely chopped.
Garnish the satay with cucumber condiment and peanuts just before serving. Serve immediately or cover and chill for no longer than 2 hours before serving.
